subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Goldilocks Zone
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Goldilocks Zone.

hook.text: The habitable zone is the distance from a star where liquid water could exist on a planet's surface. Not too hot, not too cold.

buildup.text: Earth sits comfortably inside the Sun's habitable zone. Venus is too close, Mars is at the outer edge.

discovery.text: Every star has its own habitable zone. Small red dwarfs have them close in; hot blue stars push them far out.

twist.text: Being in the zone doesn't guarantee habitability. Venus is nearly in it but has a runaway greenhouse making it 460°C.

climax.text: The concept guides target selection for missions like JWST and future telescopes searching for biosignatures.

punchline.text: Not too hot, not too cold. But being in the zone is just the beginning.

quiz.question: Why doesn't being in the habitable zone guarantee a planet is habitable?
options: Other factors like atmosphere and greenhouse effects determine surface conditions
The habitable zone only applies to gas giants, not rocky planets
Stars in the habitable zone eventually destroy their planets
